presets.name: Horse/Cow Rain Scald (Dermatophilosis) (C)"
presets.description: Horse/Cow Rain Scald (Dermatophilosis) for Contact  Dermatophilosis, commonly referred to as rain scald in horses and rain rot in cattle, is an exudative pustular dermatitis caused by the gram-positive actinomycete bacterium Dermatophilus congolensis. The disease is strongly associated with wet environmental conditions that macerate the skin, activating infective zoospores which then penetrate and colonize the living epidermis, leading to characteristic thick, paintbrush-like scabs formed from accumulated exudate, keratin, and hair. Clinical signs include painful crusty lesions often along the back, rump, and lower limbs, alopecia upon scab removal revealing raw underlying skin, pruritus, and in severe or widespread cases, fever, lethargy, and reduced performance or weight gain. The pathophysiology involves bacterial filamentous growth and division within the epidermis, triggering intense neutrophilic inflammation with microabscess formation, persistent infection protected beneath scabs, and potential chronicity with repeated wetting cycles, though the bacterium does not typically invade deeper tissues unless immunocompromised.  The proposed polypeptides target direct bactericidal mechanisms against gram-positive actinomycetes and augmentation of cutaneous innate immunity, essential for interrupting infection at the skin surface where conventional antibiotics may have limited penetration under scabs. The real-world LL-37, the mature human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide, demonstrates potent membrane-disrupting activity against gram-positive bacteria in cutaneous models, suitable for topical veterinary application to clear superficial infections. The real-world human {beta}-defensin-2 provides complementary gram-positive targeting through pore formation and immunomodulatory recruitment of immune effectors to the lesion site. The designed truncated LL-37 core, rationally derived from the central amphipathic helical segment with N-acetylation and C-amidation for enhanced protease resistance and pharmacokinetic profile, preserves bactericidal efficacy while optimizing drug-likeness for topical delivery. The designed retrocyclin-mimetic, based on the theta-defensin cyclic scaffold with disulfide bonds for structural rigidity, delivers robust broad-spectrum activity including against resistant gram-positive organisms via multiple membrane and intracellular targets. These polypeptides collectively offer multimodal, host-inspired topical interventions with advantageous physicochemical traits (high cationic charge for bacterial selectivity, amphipathicity for membrane interaction, stability enhancements) feasible for veterinary use in managing or preventing dermatophilosis outbreaks. presets.name: Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome (C)"
presets.description: Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome for Contact  Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome (HLHS) is a severe congenital heart defect involving underdevelopment of the left ventricle, mitral/aortic valves, and aorta, requiring staged surgical interventions and associated with right ventricular strain, heart failure risk, and need for regenerative support. The proposed polypeptides target evidence-based axes from cardiac repair, developmental regeneration, cytoprotection, angiogenesis, tissue remodeling, and innate repair studies to potentially enhance myocardial function, vascularization, and tissue protection as adjuncts to surgery.  1. Full-length thymosin {beta}4-mediated epicardial progenitor activation, vasculogenesis, and regeneration relevant to congenital heart development: Thymosin_beta_4 2. Acetylated actin-binding fragment for cardiomyocyte migration, anti-apoptosis, and repair in stressed myocardium: TB-500 3. Stable gastric pentadecapeptide for cytoprotection, angiogenesis, and recovery from cardiovascular lesions: BPC-157 4. Copper-bound tripeptide for ECM remodeling, growth factor induction, and antioxidant effects in cardiac tissue: GHK-Cu 5. Pyrrolidine-modified EPO-derived peptide for innate repair receptor activation and cardiac tissue protection: ARA-290 6. Optimized helical VEGF-mimetic for targeted pro-angiogenic signaling to support vascular growth in hypoplastic structures: Ac-KLTWQELYQLKYKGI-NH2 (engineered)  The Amplitude of low frequencies is automatically reduced to prevent painful sensations. presets.name: Keratomas (C)"
presets.description: Keratomas for Contact  Keratomas are rare benign tumors in the equine hoof capsule characterized by abnormal proliferation of keratin-producing cells, resulting in a mass that causes mechanical pressure, hoof distortion, bone resorption, lameness, and risk of secondary infections.  1. Multi-tissue healing: BPC-157 - Stable gastric pentadecapeptide accelerating angiogenesis, collagen organization, and repair in traumatized or chronically irritated hoof tissue. 2. Cytoskeletal remodeling: TB-500 - Synthetic thymosin beta-4 derivative promoting actin regulation, cell migration, and rapid wound healing in laminar and hoof wall structures. 3. Copper-mediated regeneration: GHK-Cu - Glycyl-histidyl-lysine copper complex stimulating coronary band activity and healthy keratin/collagen synthesis for hoof integrity. 4. Antimicrobial barrier reinforcement: LL-37 - Cathelicidin peptide providing broad-spectrum defense against bacterial and fungal invasion in hoof separations. 5. Potent anti-inflammation: KPV - Alpha-MSH-derived tripeptide suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines to alleviate pain and swelling in affected feet. 6. Tissue protection and pain relief: ARA-290 - Erythropoietin analog reducing ischemia-reperfusion injury and neuropathic pain associated with chronic lameness. 7. Immune system enhancement: Thymosin_alpha_1 - Thymic peptide boosting innate and adaptive immunity to counter persistent irritation or infection. 8. Epithelial bioregulation: Pinealon - Glu-Asp-Arg tripeptide normalizing gene expression and cellular function in hyperproliferative hoof keratinocytes. 9. Telomere/cellular longevity: Epithalon - Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly tetrapeptide supporting proper cell division and differentiation in keratin-producing tissues. 10. Integrin antagonism (designed): GRGDSP - Synthetic RGD-containing hexapeptide competitively inhibiting aberrant integrin-mediated adhesion and proliferation in keratoma cells. presets.name: Rabbit Hemorrhagic Disease Virus 2 (RHDV2) (C)"
presets.description: Rabbit Hemorrhagic Disease Virus 2 (RHDV2) for Contact  RHDV2 is a highly contagious calicivirus causing acute liver failure, disseminated intravascular coagulation, and high mortality in rabbits.   Polypeptides target key aspects such as viral entry via nucleolin binding inhibition, induction of humoral immunity through epitope presentation and dendritic cell targeting, inhibition of viral protease to block replication, disruption of viroporin function to prevent ion imbalance, hepatoprotection to mitigate necrosis, anticoagulation to reduce hemorrhage, RNA polymerase inhibition to halt genome replication, and VPg interference to disrupt viral protein-genome linkage. Designed polypeptides enhance coverage where real-world options are limited, incorporating non-standard residues for improved stability and specificity. presets.name: Rabbit Liver Torsion (C)"
presets.description: Rabbit Liver Torsion for Contact  Rabbit liver torsion, also known as liver lobe torsion, is a veterinary emergency in rabbits where one or more liver lobes twist around their vascular pedicle, resulting in occlusion of blood flow, ischemia, congestion, necrosis, and potential systemic complications like shock or coagulopathy. It most commonly affects the caudate lobe, presenting with acute anorexia, lethargy, hunched posture, reduced fecal output, and abdominal pain, often requiring prompt surgical intervention via lobectomy for optimal survival, though medical supportive care is used in some cases.   The selected polypeptides address all therapeutic axes including anti-inflammatory (e.g., TLR4 and NF-kB inhibition), anti-apoptotic (e.g., Bcl2 upregulation and Bax downregulation), hepatoprotective against necrosis, ischemia-reperfusion injury protection (e.g., reducing oxidative stress and cytokine release), vascular remodeling and angiogenesis, fibrosis suppression, and macrophage modulation (e.g., polarization to anti-inflammatory phenotypes); real-world polypeptides are prioritized from verifiable sources in hepatic IRI models applicable to torsion pathophysiology, with designed polypeptides (flagged as designed) supplementing to meet minimums and ensure full axis coverage, considering physicochemical properties such as stability, solubility, and potential for systemic or targeted delivery. presets.name: Sheep Foot Rot (C)"
presets.description: Sheep Foot Rot for Contact  Sheep foot rot (ovine footrot) is a contagious polymicrobial bacterial infection primarily caused by Dichelobacter nodosus in synergy with Fusobacterium necrophorum, leading to interdigital necrosis, hoof horn separation, severe lameness, reduced productivity, and major welfare concerns in flocks. Antimicrobial peptides provide targeted bactericidal alternatives to antibiotics, focusing on membrane disruption to combat resistance and enable topical application. 1. Native cathelicidin defense: SMAP-29 - Ovine-derived alpha-helical cathelicidin that rapidly permeabilizes gram-negative bacterial membranes with species-relevant potency. 2. Beta-hairpin pore formation: Protegrin-1 - Rigid disulfide-stabilized structure forms stable pores in bacterial membranes for broad-spectrum killing. 3. Tryptophan-rich translocation: Indolicidin - Short amphipathic peptide penetrates cells to disrupt intracellular targets alongside membrane damage. 4. Beta-defensin mimicry: Human Beta-Defensin 3 (hBD-3) - Host-like cationic peptide with strong activity against gram-negative and anaerobic pathogens via membrane attack. 5. Arginine-rich outer membrane targeting: Tachyplesin I - Disulfide-constrained beta-sheet peptide excels at disrupting gram-negative envelopes. 6. Stabilized helical optimization: OviFoot-1 - Designed truncated ovine cathelicidin analog with enhanced protease resistance for sustained topical bactericidal efficacy against hoof pathogens. presets.name: Sheep Foot Scald (C)"
presets.description: Sheep Foot Scald for Contact  Sheep foot scald (interdigital dermatitis) is a common bacterial infection in sheep caused primarily by *Fusobacterium necrophorum*, often triggered by wet, muddy conditions that macerate the skin between the hoof claws, leading to inflammation, pain, and lameness.  1. Ruminant-derived antimicrobial: Lactoferricin B - Hydrolyzes bacterial cell walls and disrupts membranes of Gram-negative pathogens prevalent in livestock. 2. Tryptophan-rich bactericidal: Indolicidin - Rapid membrane penetration and intracellular targeting of Gram-negative and Gram-positive bacteria. 3. Disulfide-stabilized broad-spectrum: Protegrin-1 - Forms oligomeric pores in bacterial membranes with high potency and environmental resilience. 4. Amphipathic pore-forming: Magainin_2 - Selectively disrupts anionic bacterial membranes via toroidal pore mechanism. 5. Alpha-helical lytic: Cecropin B - Targets prokaryotic membranes causing leakage and cell death with low eukaryotic toxicity. 6. Tissue repair modulation: Thymosin_beta_4 - Accelerates epithelial migration, angiogenesis, and anti-inflammatory resolution in damaged interdigital skin. 7. Synthetic amphipathic: VetAMP-1 - Engineered repeating Arg-Trp motif with N-acetylation and C-amidation for protease resistance and enhanced topical bactericidal activity.
